steps.reveal: tradendus = about to be handed over / must be handed over

hints.perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle would be 'traditus' — 'tradendus' with -ndus is the gerundive
hints.present active participle: Present active participle would be 'tradens' — 'tradendus' is the gerundive
hints.future active participle: Future active would be 'traditurus' — 'tradendus' with -ndus is passive, the gerundive

steps.reveal: tradendus est = must be / is about to be handed over

hints.simple future (will be): Simple future would use 'tradetur' — gerundive + 'est' expresses necessity
hints.past action (was handed over): Past would use 'traditus est' — 'tradendus est' is the passive periphrastic
hints.possibility (might be): Possibility would use subjunctive — gerundive + 'est' expresses obligation

hints.to lead across: That would be 'traducere' — 'tradere' means 'to hand over/betray'
hints.to give back: That would be 'reddere' — 'tradere' means 'to hand over/betray'
hints.to teach: That would be 'docere' — though 'tradere' can mean 'to transmit (teaching)'

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead)
hints.reddo, reddere, reddidi, redditum: These are the parts of 'reddere' (to give back)
hints.credo, credere, credidi, creditum: These are the parts of 'credere' (to believe)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'Filium' — 'Filius' is nominative subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'Fili' — 'Filius' is nominative subject

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'homo' — 'hominis' with -is is genitive singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'hominis' ends in -is

hints.nominative: While 'manus' is nominative singular, after 'in' + acc. it is accusative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive singular would also be 'manus' (4th decl.), but 'in' + acc. = 'into'
hints.dative: Dative would be 'manibus' — 'manus' after 'in' is accusative plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'manus' has gen. -ūs
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'manus' has gen. -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'manus' has gen. -ūs

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'homines' — 'hominum' is genitive plural
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'homines' — 'hominum' with -um is genitive plural
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'hominibus' — 'hominum' is genitive plural

translation: The Son of Man must be handed over into the hands of men
